asset_name: UI strip — score, depth, HP, and BPM HUD anchors
shared_properties: The strip spans the screen width and remains non-blocking for the main playfield.
Anchor positions stay fixed so text systems can update values without layout drift.
Warm cream text cues remain readable over both bright sky areas and darker tree silhouettes.
differences: This HUD strip defines the live metric anchors for score, depth, HP, and BPM.
The visual treatment is lighter than the rhythm lane panel so the top of the screen stays airy.
Three HP wells reinforce the stage start maximum health of 3 immediately.

context.usage: Top-screen HUD strip containing score, depth, HP hearts, and the lower-right BPM label anchors.

shape.silhouette: Minimal HUD guide strip with left and right text anchors plus three HP icon wells.

acceptance_criteria: PNG canvas is exactly 1280x48px and keeps all decorative shapes away from the text baseline zones.
Three HP icon wells are evenly spaced and large enough for 24x24px leaf-heart icons.
Score and depth anchor bands remain readable over the sky and tree layers without adding heavy panel weight.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the core loop, timing windows, obstacle pacing, UI layout, and Bevy target constraints.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the sepia autumn atmosphere, tree silhouettes, and warm color layering.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteBundle and text-anchor guide used by the HUD layout system for all live run metrics.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load ui/ui_hud_strip_score_depth_hp_bpm_1280x48.png during UI boot and pair it with text entities for live numeric updates and HP icon slots.
runtime_notes: Anchor score at x10 y10, HP at x10 y35, depth at the top-right, and BPM at the lower-right according to the GDD layout.
Reserve icon wells for three HP leaves so health changes never move the score text baseline.
Keep the strip art lightweight so number rendering and combo updates remain visually dominant.
